metadata.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF MOTIVATIONAL EXERCISES IN DEVELOPING STUDENTS' CREATIVE ABILITIES
metadata.description: This article analyzes the role and significance of motivational exercises in developing students’ creative abilities from a scientific and pedagogical perspective. Based on motivational theories, particularly the Self-Determination Theory developed by Deci and Ryan and Maslow’s Hierarchy of Needs, the methodology of applying motivational exercises in the educational process is discussed. The research findings confirm that motivational exercises have a positive impact on the development of students’ creative thinking, imagination, and independent reasoning.

creators.name: Gaipova, Sadoqat Qo'zibayevna
creators.affiliation: Second-Year Master's Student in Pedagogy, Nukus State Pedagogical Institute named after Ajiniyaz

keywords: creative ability, motivation, motivational exercises, creative thinking, educational process, pedagogical technology.
references: 1.Xoliqov, A., & Hamidov, O. Pedagogical Psychology. Tashkent: O'zbekiston Publishing House, 2019.
2.Yusupova, M. Developing Creative Thinking in Primary School Students. Tashkent: Fan Publishing House, 2020.
3.Lev S. Vygotsky. Imagination and Creativity in Childhood. Moscow: Prosveshcheniye, 1997.
Edward L. Deci & Richard M. Ryan. Intrinsic Motivation and Self-Determination in Human Behavior. New York: Plenum Press, 1985.
5.Abraham Maslow. Motivation and Personality. New York: Harper & Row, 1954.

journal.title: International journal of technology and academic research

data.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF MOTIVATIONAL EXERCISES IN DEVELOPING STUDENTS' CREATIVE ABILITIES
